title: Steiropteris fendleri
intro: Steiropteris fendleri, also known as the Fendler's tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Steiropteris fendleri contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Steiropteris fendleri is traditionally used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, as well as extracts, which are used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate various inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Steiropteris fendleri belongs to the family Diaphleaceae and is a type of fern. It is classified as Steiropteris fendleri within the genus Steiropteris. The plant's classification also includes it being a member of the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification places it within the subclass Polypodiidae and the order Polypodiales. At the kingdom level, Steiropteris fendleri is classified as Plantae.
